top_signals.reason: Boeing holds two NASA contracts totaling over $32.9B, both triggering the >$1B rule (+15 each) and cross-reference bonus (+25) with 29 corruption indicators flagged on both. Combined signals with multiplier produce a strong institutional tailwind score warranting a BUY consideration.

Howard Lutnick confirmed in Paradise Papers at 100% match score triggering +30pts offshore confirmed bonus. Cabinet-level figure with offshore entity raises conflict-of-interest flag but no direct contract or trade signal to cross-reference.

Bechtel is privately held so no tradeable ticker exists; the $17.2B DOE contract triggers the >$1B bonus but zero social mentions and zero corruption indicators limit actionability. No publicly traded proxy identified.

Three cybersecurity-related bills advancing through House committees signal sector tailwind for energy and pipeline cybersecurity vendors. No direct contract or trade data to cross-reference limits score ceiling.
Rex Tillerson confirmed at 100% match in Paradise Papers but holds no current cabinet or congressional role reducing signal weight. Insufficient cross-reference indicators to elevate score.

Boozman purchased Treasury bond ETFs (IEF and IEI) on the same date, suggesting a defensive rotation out of equities (IVW sold March 2025). Multiple same-senator signals apply multiplier but all are macro ETFs with no insider-specific edge.

Paired Treasury ETF purchase alongside IEF on the same date signals a coordinated defensive fixed-income shift. Multiplier applied for multiple signals same senator but macro ETF reduces actionability.
Iran escalation rhetoric from Trump family insider suggests potential oil supply disruption risk. Signal is weak without a confirmed trade or contract cross-reference to elevate conviction.
Senate sale of eToro at meaningful size warrants attention as a congressional signal. No cross-references or contract links elevate this further; base congressional score only.
Small congressional purchase of First Citizens Bancorporation with no supporting signals or cross-references. Low conviction trade at minimal size.
Small congressional purchase of JPMorgan with no corroborating signals or cross-references. Insufficient evidence to act on alone.
Moderate congressional purchase of PepsiCo with no supporting contract or cross-reference signals. Likely routine portfolio allocation.
Sale of S&P 500 Growth ETF is consistent with broader Boozman defensive rotation pattern seen later in the year. Small size limits signal strength.
Small purchase of Eaton Corp with no contract or cross-reference support. Minimal size and no corroborating signals limit actionability.

Hecla Mining active lobbying in Q2 2026 may signal anticipation of favorable mining or critical minerals legislation. No critical minerals data or contract award present to trigger cross-reference bonus.
